steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The clause before the blank (“Metamorphoses, an epic poem of Roman origin, dates back to the [BLANK]”) is an independent clause. The clause after (“the poem's heroic narrative still captivates modern readers”) is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: To join two independent clauses, use a comma followed by a coordinating conjunction. “Yet” is a coordinating conjunction that expresses the contrast between the poem's ancient origin and its modern appeal. Choice B correctly applies this structure with “1st century, yet”. Choices A and C lack the conjunction entirely, while choice D misplaces the comma. That matches choice B — B is correct.
